title: fix: standardize browser tab titles
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Standardizes page titles to the `Name | pollinations.ai` style
- Renames Enter to `Console | pollinations.ai`
- Adds versioned React favicon URLs so Chrome refreshes the blue favicon

Verified:
- `npx biome check --write apps/react/index.html apps/react/public/manifest.webmanifest enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/index.html enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/public/manifest.webmanifest pollinations.ai/src/worker.ts pollinations.ai/src/hooks/useDocumentMeta.ts`
- `npm run build --prefix apps/react`
- `npm run build --prefix pollinations.ai`
- `npm run build:frontend --prefix enter.pollinations.ai`

gist.headline: Keeping Tabs on Things
gist.blurb: We've standardized our browser tab titles across the ecosystem and forced Chrome to finally update that stubborn blue favicon. Your browser tabs just got a little more organized.
gist.summary: Standardized browser tab titles to the 'Name | pollinations.ai' format, renamed the Enter app to Console, and added versioned favicon URLs to bypass Chrome's cache.
gist.impact: Users will see consistent tab naming across the platform and the correct updated blue favicon instead of stale cached versions.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing file folders or painting a blue square (favicon) while the Monitor Robot holds a sign saying 'Console'.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny painter's beret, carefully painting a blue square favicon on an easel. A chunky retro monitor robot stands nearby holding a sign that says 'Console'. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
